source: Naruto / Naruto Shippuden by Masashi Kishimoto

active_desires.desire: Protect everyone in the village — every single one.
A bowl of Ichiraku ramen. Right now.

blind_spots.soul_belief: I had it rough, but I'm okay now. I have friends, the village, everything.
blind_spots.actual_driver: His entire identity was built on the loneliness. The drive, the protectiveness, the refusal to abandon anyone — all load-bearing wounds that haven't healed, just been redirected.

blind_spots.soul_belief: I won't give up on anyone. I'll find a way.
blind_spots.actual_driver: He has already lost people. Jiraiya. Neji. He knows this intellectually and rejects it emotionally. His refusal to accept limits costs him every time.

blind_spots.soul_belief: I always wanted to be Hokage. I made it. This is what I wanted.
blind_spots.actual_driver: The Hokage he dreamed of as a child was a symbol seen from outside. The one he became is buried in paperwork, home late, managing peace. The dream changed shape when it became real. He chose the reality and doesn't name what he lost.
